sn12.48:1.0.1: At Sāvatthī. Then a brahmin who was a cosmologistapproached the Blessed One … and said to him:
sn12.48:1.0.2: "How is it, Master Gotama: does all exist?" "‘All exists’: this, brahmin, is the oldest cosmology."
sn12.48:1.0.3: "Then, Master Gotama, does all not exist?" "‘All does not exist’: this, brahmin, is the second cosmology."
sn12.48:1.0.4: "How is it, Master Gotama: is all a unity?""‘All is a unity’: this, brahmin, is the third cosmology."
sn12.48:1.0.5: "Then, Master Gotama, is all a plurality?""‘All is a plurality’: this, brahmin, is the fourth cosmology.
sn12.48:1.0.6: Without veering towards either of these extremes, the Tathāgata teaches the Dhamma by the middle … ."
sn12.48:1.0.7: When this was said, that brahmin said to the Blessed One: "Magnificent, Master Gotama! … From today let Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life."
